commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=489a8ec5b20fc8dbcdd3b0aaad7ee7e28856680d)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds an age-only fallback in `pruneDiagnosticSessionStates()` plus unit coverage so non-idle diagnostic session entries older than 30 minutes are pruned.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +80. Total +84 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows failed recovery outcomes return before mutating diagnostic state, while current main and `v2026.6.6` only TTL-prune idle empty entries; I did not run a live gateway repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers should choose the canonical recovery-aware cleanup among the linked PRs and decide whether any fixed-TTL override is intentional before this branch proceeds.

target.title: fix(logging): prune non-idle stale diagnostic session entries
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- pruneDiagnosticSessionStates() only deletes entries where state.state === "idle" AND queueDepth <= 0 AND age > TTL (30 min).
- Entries that never transition back to "idle" after failed recovery attempts accumulate indefinitely in the module-level diagnosticSessionStates Map, holding activity snapshots, tool call history, and queue metadata in V8 heap.
- When requestStuckSessionRecovery() aborts a stalled embedded run and the recovery itself fails (e.g. session already closed, generation check fails), the outcome { status: "failed" } skips mutation — the entry stays at whatever non-idle state it had permanently.

Why does this matter now?

- Ghost entries accumulate until the 2000-entry FIFO cap is hit, wasting V8 heap.
- The prune poll interval is 60s, so stale entries can sit in memory for the entire gateway lifetime.

What is the intended outcome?

- Any entry older than SESSION_STATE_TTL_MS (30 min) is deleted regardless of state.
- Ghost entries from failed recovery attempts are cleaned up on the next prune cycle.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No changes to the stuck-session recovery logic itself.
- No changes to SESSION_STATE_TTL_MS, SESSION_STATE_PRUNE_INTERVAL_MS, or SESSION_STATE_MAX_ENTRIES constants.

## Linked context

Closes #91697

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Non-idle ghost diagnostic session entries accumulate indefinitely after failed recovery attempts.
- Real environment tested: Windows 10, Node.js v22.11.0, OpenClaw latest main (19130e0dc2).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Read src/logging/diagnostic-session-state.ts and confirmed the fallback deletion condition is added after the existing idle gate (lines 58-66).
  - Ran regression test: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/logging/diagnostic-session-state.test.ts
- Evidence after fix: Regression test output:
  Tests 3 passed (3)
  removes idle entries older than TTL
  removes non-idle stale entries older than TTL (regression #91697)
  does not remove non-idle entries within TTL
- Observed result after fix: Ghost entries stuck at "processing" or "waiting" state are cleaned up after TTL expires.
- What was not tested: Live gateway test with actual stuck session recovery failure.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The regression test uses in-memory Map manipulation; it does not prove the gateway recovery path directly.

## Tests and validation

- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/logging/diagnostic-session-state.test.ts — 3/3 passed

## Risk checklist

- userVisible: No (internal diagnostic state management)
- configEnvMigration: No
- securityAuthNetwork: No
- Mitigation: Only adds fallback cleanup; existing idle entry behavior unchanged.

## Current review state

- Pre-existing CI failures (not from this PR): checks-node-core-runtime-infra-process runs src/process/**/*.test.ts which is unrelated to our change in src/logging/diagnostic-session-state.ts.
- Ready for review after this update is pushed.
- [x] AI-assisted (built with Claude Code)
